speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys, most of you, other than the twins, head into the magnificent mansion. An evening that is restful by definition but still wracked with tension and anxiety. You guys watch throughout the night, relatively uneventful, though a cool evening. Without building a fire, you both do have to keep bundled up against the elements. It's not cold enough to the point where it's going to be impactful, but it is chilly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you dart back and slowly return to your group, glancing over in that direction, the patrol doesn't seem to take notice and continues walking past.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So am I the only one that, while we wait, is itching for a fight?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry, look, it's just who I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well listen, if you want a fight, we could still use one of their heads. We could find one of those patrolmen walking around, grab him, take his head.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did we not just learn anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're going to be fighting soon enough. I think everyone should hide inside your love shack and my sister and I should wait here in the grass and watch and wait. And when the dragon comes, we alert you all.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We wait for a dragon pass. The day after the dragon passes, we strike.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As the evening progresses, you guys rest, preparing yourselves for the next day. You guys--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I close the door, I open it, and I read it. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm so proud of you. I'm so proud of you!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nobody look, nobody look. Don't you look! Don't you look. Don't you look.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You know what? I love her anyway. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: This is a bit of tome, so I think I should wait to read this, but a part of me-- Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I want to know what it says so fucking bad!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We got to wait for her to finish, because the night is young.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The cube. Over and over and over again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the meantime, while you're reading that, you guys are doing what?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We were strategizing. We were just, we're figuring out some strategy for how to make this work.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Right away, sir." And they drift off to prepare your bountiful breakfast. Eventually you go ahead and begin to muster the rest of your party down towards the breakfast area, and the rest of you come to consciousness and find your way to where the food is being served and Vex and Vax both wait, rather stone-faced, and prepared to speak.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It seems like a slim thread of hope, but perhaps the underlings are not entirely satisfied with this arrangement? Just scooped one up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's possible. I have a feeling they're having a hard time finding any sort of hoard to offer after this many days.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a long shot, but you told me about those giants.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: When you were with Zahra, and they turned. We shouldn't rely on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's possible. If Grog can take control of the Herd, they may be able to fight with us. But I wouldn't trust them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We need to tell the others.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys find your way back to the door, entering the mansion. Servants have already, at the sound of you entering, begin to appear out of their spectral, ethereal states, bowing to you, offering to take your coat.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You keep moving towards the very, very center of town, moving past a couple of other patrols. You've passed, even in just this journey into the center of-- journey to the center of Westruun-- you've passed roughly 30 or so well-armed, relatively savage individuals that mark the same civilized level of Grog, give or take some further steps into society. Many of them are wearing pieces of clothing that have been taken from this town, they're dressed better than their station, you know, rough-looking, big, thick, unshaven faces for the humans, but they're still like dressed in pompous clothing that's partially torn and smeared. They're a walking parody of the people that once ruled this town, and they seem to know it. They're enjoying this gloating. Eventually you make your way to the central town square, which is not far from where the Margrave's home is. This area, you do notice, has been set up as a defensive central location. There are just barriers and barricades of all sorts of furniture that have been nailed together and this has been made an assault post, where if anyone were to attempt to attack the center of this town, this would be a wonderful defensive position for someone to hole up and wait it out, whether it be against other people or a dragon. It has been built with the best of their ability in that regard. And there are a number of weapons that they've taken from the city that have been bolted in, that have been strapped around, so there's just like jagged edges of halberds and whatever blades they could muster in every direction, therefore if anything wasn't being very careful and running through here, they could be easily shoved into one of these hazards or perhaps even pushed onto it as a means of display. Which you do notice, on a number of these, various heads are currently placed as warning symbols, in varying states of decay. You recognize two of the heads. One of which is--
trigger_phrase: you make your way to the central town

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 19. You glance by what you knew previously in Westruun as the central church of Erathis there. It's a rather, in comparison to the rest of the town, a gaudy temple. But it has been defiled fairly heavily. You can see paint, mud, or some other substance has been smeared across parts of it, portions of the rock have been pushed over and collapsed. You do glance and see a small humanoid peeking through one of the ajar doors that you see, for a second you see a set of eyes, then it sees you and closes the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. Fuck, he knows I'm there, I got to keep going. I got to keep going, yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: They themselves look a little worse for wear, and he looks over his shoulder and says, "Well shit, Kaylie, weren't you just talking about him yesterday?" As your daughter stands behind him, holding two of the children, trying to protect them as you appeared. She looks up at you and goes, "Oh, you've got to be kidding me." And that's where we'll end tonight's game. (yelling)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As Dr. Dranzel stands there before you. (cheering)
